summary: A clause's compiled label becomes its address â€” deterministic, human-legible, written once at emit, printed by every finding and by `explain`; two rows under one label are a malformed lock

edit.description: `ClauseRow` (2627) carries kind/predicate/field/severity/guidance/cite and per-predicate argument columns â€” **no label column exists**, re-verified on disk at 399d8e3. It gains one, written once at emit (843) as the row's identity. This is the entry's core: the label is not a spelling widened but a column added.

`Selector::rule` (348-353) is today's printed clause id and returns the bare predicate key (`max_len`) or `requirement.<key>` â€” not unique per row, since two clauses of one predicate over different fields of one kind collide. Widen it to the deterministic per-row address the lock writes; `finding` (548) already routes it onto every diagnostic. One identity per row: the label IS the rule id, never a second human-facing name beside it (0032's rejected alternative; `specs/process/engineering.md`, "One job, one home").
`Diagnostic::rule` (39-40, doc'd `skill.name-format` â€” stable, the diagnostic `code`) is the one home for the printed id; its doc states the label grammar.
The clause-row read-back (`clause_from_row`, 176-190) lifts the label off the row; the engine treats it as opaque and never resolves a collision.
`explain` (208) prints each clause's label â€” see the annoying finding, read its label, dial it.

Admissibility refuses two rows wearing one label â€” a malformed lock, rejected before it judges anything (`contract.md`, "well-formedness"). Sibling of three shipped coherence rules, none covering clause rows: `KIND_COLLISION_RULE` (1786), `LOCAL_LOCUS_RULE` (1860, new at 40619a0), `GOVERNS_COLLISION_RULE` (1902). The `engine::admissibility` calls at 811/850 are the path.
The ts-rs seam row, regenerated â€” do not hand-edit.

schemaDelta: ClauseRow gains a `label` column â€” the compiled address, written once at emit.

tests.asserts: every emitted clause row carries a deterministic, human-legible label; the same program emits the same labels; a lock carrying two rows under one label fails admissibility loud
a clause finding prints its label as the diagnostic code, and `explain` narrates the same label for the same clause

acceptance: Every clause declaration row has a documented, deterministic, human-legible address; every finding and `explain` print it; a colliding lock is refused (contract.md, "clause"; pipeline.md, "The lock"). `cargo test` green.
notes: 0032 assumed this shipped ('the lock already writes one per row and refuses collisions'); on disk it does not â€” the founding corpus claimed the label at e842a32 and no entry ever built it. So the work is the column plus the grammar plus the refusal, not a spelling change. **Ungated 07-16**: LOCAL-LOCUS-COMMITMENT-CLASS shipped (40619a0), and it was the sole blocker. The dial cannot name a clause until this lands. scoped at f1d97e4; cites re-read at 399d8e3.

summary: A policy layer is a lock the invocation joins: `check --layer <path>` joins named locks' rows over this corpus, kinds travel by name, and a joined lock failing admissibility fails the check

edit.description: The `Check` command (105-122: `root`, `harness`, `deny_advisories`, `reporter`) gains a repeatable `--layer <path>`. Each named lock's declaration rows join over the host corpus: kinds travel by name, the joined clauses range over the host's selections. Fail-closed costs nothing new â€” a joined lock that fails admissibility is already a malformed lock, refused loud through the existing `engine::admissibility` path (811/850). 0030's uncommitted rules bind the join: hardening is unbounded in every mode, softening is visible-only and gate-inert.

`read_declarations` (3104) / `parse_declarations` (3127) read one lock today; the join reads the invocation's locks the same way â€” the lock stays the one interchange, and no second parser is minted beside this one.
Clause rows compose into the host's selections whatever lock carried them; a joined row is an ordinary row.

tests.asserts: `check --layer <lock>` joins the named lock's clauses over the host corpus and gates on them; a joined lock that fails admissibility fails the check loud; a joined clause that would soften the host's gate is inert in block mode while a hardening one binds

acceptance: An org authors an ordinary temper corpus, emits its lock, and distributes it; a consumer joins it at check and is gated by it, fail-closed (pipeline.md, "Layers"). `cargo test` green.
notes: The third of 0032's five. Org-side authoring stays TS and the lock stays the one interchange â€” no new format, no trust model, no payload-as-input (0032 rejects that by name: it breaks offline and the payload's internality). Serialized behind the label entry: both edit `src/main.rs`, `src/compose.rs`, `src/drift.rs`. scoped at f1d97e4; cites re-read at 399d8e3.

summary: The `type` predicate accepts a set of lattice kinds â€” the six documented `string|array(|object)` manifest fields become gateable, and a one-element set is today's behavior exactly

edit.description: `Predicate::Type` (97-102) carries one `kind: ValueType` (101); it becomes a set, satisfied when the field's preserved kind is any member. The variant's doc (93-96) states the widening. `predicate_from_row` (321) decodes the column. An empty set admits nothing and is inadmissible â€” the inverted-bound precedent `range` and `count` already set.

The `Type` arm (626) matches the feature's preserved kind against one declared kind; it becomes set membership, and the violation message names the declared set. `admissibility` (110) rejects the empty set beside the existing vacuous-clause refusals â€” the empty `forbidden_keys` case (220) is the shape to follow.

`ClauseRow::value_type` (2672) is `Option<String>` â€” one lattice name â€” written at 3511-3512 and read at 3553; it carries the set. An older lock's bare string reads as a one-element set and the next emit rewrites the file whole in canonical form: the robust read plus canonical rewrite an upgraded engine owes a committed lock (`pipeline.md`, "The lock"), never a patch of the file.
The three row-build sites carrying `value_type: None` (226, 253, 275) move with the column.

The `Type` arm (64-67) writes JSON Schema's `type` from one lattice kind via `json_type`; a set is JSON Schema's own type-array, so the `schema` verb keeps saying exactly what the clause says and nothing more.
`Predicate.value_type` (43-47, spelled to match the lock's column) and the `type` ctor (74) take the set; a one-element call stays today's behavior exactly.

`pluginManifestDefaultContract`'s header (606-620) names two holds; the second (615-620, "Wrong-typed component paths") discharges â€” `skills`, `commands`, `agents`, `hooks`, `mcpServers`, `lspServers` gain `type` clauses over their documented unions, each carrying a fresh raw cite retrieved at encode time, never re-encoded from this description. The first hold (609-614) survives and stays named: `closed-keys` is the widening that closes it.
The ts-rs seam row, regenerated â€” do not hand-edit; `tests/seam_bindings_current.rs` holds it current.
Generated data, never transcribed â€” re-derived from the SDK module by the frozen lane (`tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs`), which byte-compares it.

The coverage oracle shipped at 97e84a1, after this entry was scoped â€” it makes this the entry that moves the measured lag, in two places. (1) `covered_rule` (135) destructures `Predicate::Type { field, kind }` and calls `json_schema_type(*kind)` (141): the set breaks that arm, which re-spells as the `type in {a|b}` form `schema_rules` already emits; its doc (132-134) calls a union-typed property "deliberately unreachable here" because a single-kind clause "would reject the documented string form" â€” that sentence retires with the widening. (2) `EXPECTED_LAG` (34) drops exactly the six rows this closes â€” `agents`, `commands`, `hooks`, `lspServers`, `mcpServers`, `skills` â€” and the header's "six union-typed component fields awaiting a `type` that accepts a set" (31-32) retires with them. `monitors`/`outputStyles`/`themes` are union-typed too and stay in the lag: they are components no clause types at all, outside builtins.md's six. `the_lag_behind_the_published_schema_is_the_named_expected_set` (181) fails loud with "re-state EXPECTED_LAG" if this is skipped, so the diff is reviewable by construction, never silent.

schemaDelta: ClauseRow's `value_type` column carries a set of lattice names rather than one; a one-element set is today's behavior, and a bare string an older lock wrote reads as a one-element set.

tests.asserts: a `type` clause over a set holds when the field carries any member kind and fails naming the whole declared set; a one-element set behaves exactly as today's single kind; an empty set is inadmissible; a lock row spelling a bare string reads as a one-element set
the measured lag behind the published schema shrinks by exactly the six union-typed component rules this closes, and the restated `EXPECTED_LAG` is what the oracle now holds
a manifest whose `skills` key carries a documented `string|array` value passes, and one carrying a number fails through the real gate

acceptance: The six documented union-typed manifest fields are gated rather than held (builtins.md, "Default contracts": a hold is named with the widening that closes it, and discharges when it ships). `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: First of 0033's four widenings (0029's move). Serialized behind CHECK-JOINS-INVOCATION-LOCKS: both edit `src/drift.rs` and `src/compose.rs`. The wave's four entries each edit `src/contract.rs`, `src/engine.rs`, `src/schema.rs`, `sdk/src/builtins.ts` and the generated seam, so they chain rather than fan out. The oracle rider is an audit finding, not a rescope: it shipped after this was scoped and now measures exactly this widening. scoped at 15b7a3a; cites re-read at 399d8e3.

summary: Field addressing widens to a declared RFC 9535 subset â€” name segments and `[*]` only, riding `serde_json_path`; anything beyond the subset is rejected at admissibility

edit.description: `serde_json_path` joins the sanctioned dependency set (0033, human-ruled; re-verify the crate live at encode time). It is hidden mechanics exactly as `regex` (40) hides under `allowed_chars` and `globset` (36) under the governs glob â€” never a hand-rolled path walker (`specs/process/engineering.md`, "Libraries before hand-rolls"). Two stale-cite riders discharge while the file is open, neither having a carrier of its own: 37-39 sells `regex` as "the `pattern` primitive", a predicate that does not exist (`rg Pattern src/contract.rs src/engine.rs` is empty) and that the corpus refuses outright â€” its live job is the charset mechanics behind `allowed_chars`; and 42-45 cites `src/schema/interchange.rs` (the module is `src/schema.rs`; no `schema/` dir exists) and assigns ts-rs the interchange-TS role the `sdk/src/generated/` seam bindings superseded (36a7662). Both re-read on disk at 399d8e3.

`FeatureValue` (102-117) flattens both containers: `List` (113) stringifies its elements and `Map` (116) carries no payload at all, so nothing can reach inside either. Extraction retains the nested values the paths evaluate over. 0033 rejects un-flattening `FeatureValue` as the fix **by name** â€” that would touch the `type` predicate's kind-preservation contract (`kind` at 108, read by the `Type` arm) and every consumer, where path evaluation over retained values needs no row change.

Path evaluation replaces bare-key lookup at the field-reading seam (`features.field(...)`, e.g. the `Type` arm at 626 and `scalar(features, field)` at 708); `[*]` is the each-grain over elements. `admissibility` (110) rejects a path using anything beyond the declared subset â€” filters, slices, recursive descent â€” so the RFC engine stays hidden and never surfaces as an author-facing pattern language (`intent.md`, invariant 1; 0033 refuses author-facing JSONPath for the third time on the record).
`field` stays a `String` on both faces â€” no row delta (0033). The docs on the field-carrying variants state the declared subset and its bound.

`push_unique(&mut required, field)` (63) and `property(&mut properties, field)` (65) treat `field` as a top-level key; `owner.name` is not one. What the `schema` verb says about a nested path is the entry's to settle and it must settle it â€” spelling a path as a top-level required key would have the editor demand a key the format never documents, a forged finding at the keystroke (`distribution.md`, "The placements and their enforcement modes": validation is the decidable predicates only).
The `field` parameters' docs (`Predicate.field` at 26-27, and the node-scope ctors from 70) state the addressing subset an author may write â€” names and `[*]`, nothing else.

`marketplaceDefaultContract`'s header (802-818) names one gap behind three bullets. Two discharge: `owner.name` required (806-809) and each `plugins[]` entry's `name`/`source` (810-812) become clauses, with fresh raw cites retrieved at encode time. The third (813-816, the `source` union) **survives and stays named**: it needs a discriminated-union predicate 0033 does not ratify, and the bullet already names that widening, which is what keeps it a hold rather than a silent absence (`builtins.md`, "Default contracts"). The `required("owner")` (866) and `required("plugins")` (872) guidance says the per-entry bar is unreachable; that sentence retires with the reach.
Generated data â€” re-derived and byte-compared by `tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs`.

schemaDelta: none â€” `field` is a string on both faces; the subset is a grammar over its contents, checked at admissibility.

tests.asserts: a clause addressing `owner.name` reads the nested value and fires on it; `plugins[*].source` evaluates each-grain over the array's elements; a path spelling a filter, a slice, or recursive descent is inadmissible rather than skipped; a bare name addresses exactly what it addresses today
a catalog whose `owner` object omits `name`, and one whose `plugins[]` entry omits `source`, each fail through the real gate

acceptance: `owner.name` and `plugins[*].source` are spellable and gated; the RFC engine is hidden mechanics and a path beyond the declared subset is refused (builtins.md, "Default contracts"). `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: 0033's second widening. Its human half rides but is not this entry's to write: CLAUDE.md's stack list is a `.temper` projection, so adding `serde_json_path` there is a `chore(harness)` act (CLAUDE.md, "The two harnesses") â€” name it in the commit body rather than editing the projection. Serialized behind TYPE-ACCEPTS-A-SET over `src/contract.rs`, `src/engine.rs`, `src/schema.rs`, `sdk/src/builtins.ts`. scoped at 15b7a3a.

summary: `closed-keys`: a clause declaring the kind's already-declared `required`/`optional` key set exhaustive â€” an undeclared key is a finding, and `plugin-manifest`'s `--strict` bar becomes spellable

edit.description: The `Predicate` enum (81) gains `ClosedKeys` â€” a variant naming no field of its own: it consumes the kind's already-declared `Required` (83-88) and `Optional` (89-94) rows as the allow-list, so the key set is never authored twice (0033). `predicate_from_row` (321) decodes it.

Evaluation reads the contract's own sibling rows rather than one field's value â€” a predicate needing wider context is evaluation cost, never a category (`contract.md`, "clause": the degree/reachability precedent). `Predicate::Optional` returns `Outcome::Holds` (621) and nothing consumes the record today; `closed-keys` is what consumes it. `admissibility` (110) rejects a `closed-keys` clause on a kind declaring no keys â€” vacuous, the shape the empty `forbidden_keys` refusal (220) already sets. The `ForbiddenKeys` arm (695) is the deny-list complement this does not replace.
`ClauseRow` (2627) needs no argument column for it â€” the allow-list derives from the kind's sibling rows, so the row is predicate/severity/guidance/cite alone.
`clause_from_row` (176-190) lifts it; the row-build sites carry no new column.

The exhaustive `match` (62) needs an arm, and `closed-keys` is the one predicate whose JSON Schema face is the whole object's rather than a property's: `additionalProperties: false`. The no-op arm list (110-120) is where a predicate lands when it has no schema face; this one has one.
A `closedKeys()` ctor joins the node-scope predicates (70-80); it names no field, and its doc says the allow-list is the kind's own declared keys, never a second list.

`pluginManifestDefaultContract`'s first hold (609-614) â€” unrecognized top-level fields, "the substance of `--strict`" â€” discharges: the manifest's documented keys already ride as `required`/`optional` rows, and one `closedKeys()` clause declares them exhaustive, with a fresh raw cite retrieved at encode time. The header's parenthetical that nothing consumes `optional` (612-613) retires with it. The shipped `forbiddenKeys` deny-list slice ("the one deny-list slice that *is* decidable") stays unless it is now subsumed â€” check on disk and say which in the commit body (`engineering.md`, "One job, one home").
The ts-rs seam row, regenerated â€” do not hand-edit.
Generated data â€” re-derived and byte-compared by `tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs`.

`covered_rule` (135) matches `Predicate` exhaustively, so a new variant is a compile error until it lands in an arm; `ClosedKeys` joins the no-op list (147-165), whose comment already reasons about the `--strict` bar. `EXPECTED_LAG` must NOT move, and that is pinned rather than asserted here: the shipped `the_published_schema_states_no_closed_key_rule_so_the_strict_bar_is_outside_this_diff` (217) reads the vendored schema's absent `additionalProperties` and states this widening "closes that hold against the *docs*, and this oracle will not move when it lands". If the lag test fires anyway, the arm is wrong, not the list.

schemaDelta: ClauseRow carries a `closed-keys` predicate key with no new argument column â€” the allow-list is the kind's own `required`/`optional` rows.

tests.asserts: a member carrying a key the kind declares neither required nor optional is a finding at the clause's declared severity; a member carrying only declared keys holds; the clause on a kind declaring no keys is inadmissible; the allow-list is read from the kind's rows, so adding an `optional` row admits the key with no second edit
a manifest carrying an undocumented top-level key fails through the real gate â€” the `--strict` bar the kind's contract mirrors

acceptance: `plugin-manifest`'s contract checks the strictest documented profile it claims â€” `claude plugin validate --strict` â€” rather than naming the gap as a hold (builtins.md, "Default contracts"). `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: 0033's third widening. `optional` has been a row nothing reads since it shipped; this is the consumer that makes it load-bearing, so the first `closed-keys` clause is also the first real test that a kind's declared rows are complete. Serialized behind FIELD-ADDRESSING-RFC-9535-SUBSET over `src/contract.rs`, `src/engine.rs`, `src/schema.rs`, `sdk/src/builtins.ts`. scoped at 15b7a3a; cites re-read at 399d8e3.

summary: `shape`: a predicate over a closed enum of engine-implemented, doc-cited shapes, opening with the two documented ones â€” the skill name's hyphen rules and no-XML-tags-in-description

edit.description: The `Predicate` enum (81) gains `Shape { field, shape }` over a new closed `Shape` enum whose members are engine-implemented and doc-cited, opening with exactly two: the skill name's hyphen rules (no leading, trailing, or consecutive hyphen) and no-XML-tags-in-description. An unknown shape is rejected at load exactly as an unknown predicate is, never skipped (`contract.md`, "clause": the vocabulary is closed and growing it is a deliberate language change) â€” `predicate_from_row` (321) is that boundary. No author ever writes a pattern: 0033 refuses the author-facing pattern clause for the third time on the record.

Each shape's mechanics are `regex`, hidden exactly as it hides under `allowed_chars` (708). The violation message names the shape and the clause's guidance teaches it. `admissibility` (110) needs no new refusal â€” an unknown shape never decodes into a clause at all.

`ClauseRow` (2627) gains a `shape` column carrying the enum member's name, following the `value_type` precedent (2672, written at 3511-3512 and read at 3553): the row family carries the argument as its name and decodes it at the boundary rather than carrying an engine type.
`clause_from_row` (176-190) and the three row-build sites (226, 253, 275) carry the column.

The exhaustive `match` (62) needs an arm, and which one is a real choice the entry must make and state: emitting a shape's hidden regex as JSON Schema's `pattern` puts a pattern in front of an author through the back door, while the no-op arm list (110-120) leaves the keystroke quieter than the gate. `distribution.md` ("The placements and their enforcement modes") bounds it â€” the generated schema is the decidable predicates only, and it is machine output, never clause surface.
A `shape()` ctor and a `Shape` string-union type join the node-scope predicates (70-80): an author picks a named shape and can spell nothing else â€” the closed enum is the type.

`skillDefaultContract`'s header (889-894) names both holds and already names this widening ("a narrow shape predicate governs additions"); both discharge â€” the name's hyphen rules and the platform's "no XML tags in the description" become clauses with fresh raw cites retrieved at encode time. The `allowedChars` name clause (914) already keeps XML out of the *name* and its guidance says so; this is the description's own bar, and the two must not double-cover the name.
The ts-rs seam row, regenerated â€” do not hand-edit.
Generated data â€” re-derived and byte-compared by `tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs`.

`covered_rule` (135) matches `Predicate` exhaustively, so `Shape` is a compile error until it lands in an arm. It belongs in the no-op list (147-165) â€” the oracle measures the plugin-manifest floor against the published schema, this widening ships clauses on `skill` alone, and the schema states no shape rule for a `type`/`minLength`-vocabulary diff to count. `EXPECTED_LAG` must not move; if it does, the arm is wrong.

schemaDelta: ClauseRow gains a `shape` column carrying the declared shape's enum name, when the predicate is `shape`.

tests.asserts: a skill name with a leading, trailing, or consecutive hyphen is a finding and a clean slug is not; a description carrying an XML tag is a finding; a lock row naming a shape the enum does not carry is refused at load rather than skipped; no clause surface accepts an author-written pattern

acceptance: The two documented decidable spec rules the skill contract has named as a hold since it shipped are gated, over a closed enum no author can extend (builtins.md, "Default contracts"). `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: 0033's fourth widening â€” the "later wave under their own design" 0022 reserved. The enum's closure is the whole safety property: it is what separates a shape from the open lint `intent.md` invariant 2 exists to prevent, so a shape lands with its doc cite or it does not land. Serialized behind CLOSED-KEYS-CLAUSE over `src/contract.rs`, `src/engine.rs`, `src/schema.rs`, `sdk/src/builtins.ts`. scoped at 15b7a3a; cites re-read at 399d8e3.

summary: Ship the temper-owned `dial` kind: a local-locus document naming clauses by label and declaring the severity this machine reads them at â€” deletion unspellable, softening inert in block mode

edit.description: `all_kinds` (374-388) ships eleven claude-code kinds; the dial joins as temper's own â€” its schema IS 0030's envelope: an entry names a clause by its compiled label and declares a severity, and the schema cannot spell deletion, so a dialed clause still reports.

The provider face. The dial is **temper-owned, not a claude-code kind**, so it exports from the root, never the `claude-code` subpath (`specs/builtins.md`: the shipped claude-code kinds export from that subpath alone).
The root export carries the dial.

`Format` (555-564) spells `yaml-frontmatter` and `json-document` â€” no TOML face exists, re-verified at 399d8e3. What the dial's document face becomes is exactly the fork this entry waits on. The locus half is no longer hypothetical: `Commitment::Local` (46-56) and `CustomKind::declare_local` (730) ship, and `local_locus_fault` (746) refuses a local kind that is not layout content â€” so a `toml-document` dial trips that fence as written, which is the fork's stake stated in code rather than in prose.
Check applies the dial's severities to the labelled clauses it names, and its softening is inert in block mode â€” so a block-mode pass on any machine implies the shared gate's pass.

schemaDelta: Pending the fork â€” the dial's document face is what `(local-locus-toml-face)` rules.

tests.asserts: a dial entry re-reads a clause at the declared severity and the clause still reports; a dial cannot spell deletion; dialed softening is inert in block mode while dialed hardening binds in every mode

acceptance: See the annoying finding, read its label, dial it â€” locally, uncommitted, visibly, and without ever softening the shared gate (contract.md, "clause"; pipeline.md, "Layers"). `cargo test` and `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Fork-gated, not merely serialized: `contract.md` calls the dial a local-locus **TOML** document while `representation.md` declares a local locus layout-only, and a layout is markdown's heading tree. Build must not pick between those sentences. Gated behind 0033's wave (shares `sdk/src/builtins.ts`): this waits on a human either way, and four ratified widenings must not wait with it. The fork is enforced code now â€” see the `src/kind.rs` note. scoped f1d97e4; re-read 399d8e3.

summary: `check` announces every active local member, every dialed clause, and every joined lock â€” the gate says out loud which inputs judged this run

edit.description: The check run announces the three uncommitted-or-joined inputs by name: each active local member, each dialed clause (by its label), each joined lock. The announcement is unconditional â€” a run judged by inputs review never saw says so, whatever the verdict.

Every reporter renders one diagnostic source (`specs/distribution.md`): `session_start` (65), `github` (141), `sarif` (172) each carry the announcement in their own shape rather than one reporter growing a private line.

tests.asserts: a check run with a local member, a dialed clause, and a joined lock announces all three; a run with none announces nothing extra

acceptance: `check` announces every active local member, every dialed clause, and every joined lock (pipeline.md, "Layers"). `cargo test` green.
notes: Last of 0032's five â€” it announces what the other four introduce, so it is honest only once they exist. Serialized behind the dial (shares `src/main.rs`) and inherits its fork through the chain. scoped at f1d97e4.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics â€” the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (â‡’ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (â‡’ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence â€” never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. All cites re-verified unmoved at f1d97e4. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (65) sits under a doc (61-64) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' â€” a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (627) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (654), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts â€” hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it â€” 525 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 624 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') â€” carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 649-652 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth').

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1356, re-verified unmoved at f1d97e4) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment â€” `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding â€” so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1357-1361) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1359) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper â€” the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at 399d8e3 and holds â€” nothing ruled the hop semantics, and `git diff 9409a6c..HEAD -- src/graph.rs tests/graph.rs` is empty, so every cite stands unmoved: the constant 5 at 65, the narration at 525/624, the BFS at 654, the boundary test at 1356. Stays parked: the constant may still be wrong. rescoped 4a33911; re-cited 53519b9; re-verified 399d8e3.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binaryâ†”npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) â€” no signing creds â€” but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at 399d8e3, holding on every clause: `git tag -l` carries only the four era tags and no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, and release.yml:7-9 still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. `git diff 9409a6c..HEAD -- .github/` is empty. scoped at b676656.
